title: Rates Term Structure x Equity Vol

entries.text: The FRED JSON feed for the ten-year minus two-year spread keeps the curve shape visible without the CSV attestation problem that blocked the first broad sweep. A current curve-shape observation is useful because it tells the reader whether the Treasury term structure is still positive, flattening, or slipping back toward stress without any narrative leap.

Cboe’s SPX delayed quote endpoint makes the flagship equity index observable in the same public JSON family as the volatility series. That is helpful because it allows a direct market-state snapshot of the underlying index without requiring a separate vendor, which keeps the follow-on vol comparison inside one consistent public source cluster.

VVIX is useful because it tracks volatility-of-volatility rather than the broad market level itself, while the Treasury curve gives a slower macro calm-versus-stress read. If VVIX is elevated while the curve remains orderly, that pairing creates a clean contradiction: option traders are paying up for instability even though the public rates structure still looks comparatively composed.
